items.label: Part A1 / A1 — Stage 0, fact-family engine integration, RDF/JSON-LD export, grammar-as-bridge
items.text:
Yes obviously stage 0. Prioritize (b) integration for fact families sooner rather than later. The engine is the project's unique strength — facts should feed it natively.
Build the derived RDF/JSON-LD export (a) in parallel as soon as fact: stabilizes. Interop is free leverage.
Treat the grammar path as a bridge, not the destination. Plan the migration path to richer EDB facts explicitly.

witnesses.note: Closed/shipped. Row 681 review-done records: Stage 0 ADOPTED; (b) fact-family engine integration prioritized; (a) derived RDF/JSON-LD export planned in parallel once fact: stabilizes; grammar path named a bridge with an explicit migration path to richer EDB facts — matches all three named A1 directives.
review-done: kr-titration-design-exploration — the disposition row itself, verbatim-matching language to row 680's A1 text.

additionally: I'll want the autoharn part of the db to be backed up before starting on this

items.label: Part B, B1 — the ADR-0017 'ratification packet' ghost referent, excavate what happened
items.text: B1 through B6 all yes. More particularly, B1 is an ADR-0017 violation and it would be interesting to figure out what happened. If our ledger is unable to excavate this (assuming it was online when these were written) then that's in itself quite damning of our harness, though we should be forgiving if the deficiency is structural -- as a matter of policy we should never rely on .claude transcripts to derogate the harness, only to diagnose it (it's use for this is sanctioned, but in the real world, end users might not have access to the transcripts and so we have to "play by the rules" instead of bending over backwards or "cheating" when thinking about the capabilities the harness is able to provide).

witnesses.note: Closed/shipped. Slug matches design/MAINT-MAINTAINER-DECISION-BRIEF-2026-07-14.md's own B1 act ('go-ahead: adr-0017-ratification-packet-referent').

Full excavation answer, ledger-only, no transcripts consulted: two-stage causation (real packet filed 2026-07-11, cited as prose not a link — an ADR-0017 Rule 2(a) violation live from birth; ledger structurally could not have caught it, its own first row postdates ratification by 4.5h — the forgivable-if-structural class the maintainer pre-named in row 684).
seam close: gates-trio SHIPPED — restates the B1 excavation answer as delivered, plus the fix (commit 9a9dd3e, 0017 packet referent + attestations).

items.text: On B2, I say "Yes please and automate it, it's pestilential and ironically visible in MAINT-MAINTAINER-DECISION-BRIEF-2026-07-14.md (Part A1 has a sub-heading called A2, meanwhile there's a "part A2" which is entirely distinct from A1.A2, if you can parse that sentence. It's ripe for confusion, just think about "telephone game" to realize how bad the situation really is.)

witnesses.note: Closed/shipped. Row 684's go-ahead names it explicitly: 'bare-P-label detector (AUTOMATE IT -- his emphasis; the brief's own A1.A2-vs-Part-A2 heading collision named as a live specimen of the confusion class, fix it)'.
seam close: gates-trio SHIPPED — f669bae bare-P-label detector + two LICENSED law one-liners + brief heading fix; gate witnessed clean corpus-wide, bare-P-label 0/35.

items.label: Concern 1 — migration path for a broken real deployment (~/ent): recovery mode, signed root provisioning + signed recovery act
items.text:
1. migration path: ~/ent being a broken installation, and *still being broken* (please send out another sonnet spy to see what has happened since last time), there should be a recovery mode,
"assurances" be damned (a malfunctioning harness will be thrown away in practice, then providing nothing at all -- it's a common theme and danger with "ceremony becomes cargo-cult"); maybe provide a
fail-hard when the initial commission (or, let's say, a root provisioning ledger entry -- and by the way, maybe this is something we need to have regardless and as a high-assurance service) has a cryptographic
signature, then requiring the "recovery operation" itself to be signed. This is distinct from the decision that "old worlds are void and dust", which was a *posture* regarding *autoharn experiments*
(which are throwaway and obviously need no backfilling or migration, or anything like that). Note that this item composes somewhat with B5 above, more or less orthogonally.

witnesses.note: Open. Design shipped (see row 714) but the buildable pieces fail hard on every invocation today because key generation remains deferred per the standing crypto ruling — honestly still open.

shipped: ORCH-DEPLOYMENT-PINNING.md + ORCH-RECOVERY-MODE-SIGNED.md (commit 4ede79c) — recovery-mode-signed design: fail-hard destructive recovery for real deployments requiring a signed root provisioning ledger entry + a separately signed recovery operation; constitutional-vs-Sonnet-buildable split named; states plainly the buildable pieces fail hard today because key generation is deferred.
Open. The concrete migration act for ~/ent itself (in-place, preserving history while gaining s29 features); Phase 0/1 rehearsals complete, Phase 2 live apply reserved to the maintainer.

ent-inplace-migration Phase 1 REHEARSAL COMPLETE: PASS on all amended-checklist items — rehearsal witnessed passing on real ent data (row 757: 1889 rows byte-identical, chain intact), nothing live touched yet.

items.label: Concern 2 — formalize the "spy" method as observability-driven development of autoharn itself
items.text:
2. on a related note, (and c.f. my note on Part A2.IR) we need to formalize the "spy" method as an observability-driven development of autoharn itself; I believe that we have already an opt-in key for
some of this type of autoharn service when provisioning autoharn for a project.

witnesses.note: Open, unclaimed. Row 688: same work item rides the IR control-family posture above; no closure or claim row found in the ledger for this slug beyond its opening — honestly OPEN.

items.label: Concern 3 — two random thoughts: defeasible epistemic logic for maintainer decisions; a FUSE VFS hydrated from a verified knowledge store
items.text:
3. Two random thoughts, somewhat mutually composable: (1) "defeasible epistemic logic" (at least on a surface reading, no idea what it would truly mean) applied to maintainer decisions, so that
orchestrator can take decisions in maintainer's absence. Take note: maintainer being human (for the time being, anyways) means it has imperfect memory and will "lie" and confabulate as a matter of course, and
that's just part of being human. Secondly, and related to the KR thread, is my idea to have a virtual file system (e.g. FUSE-mounted) with structured files hydrated from a verified knowledge store. In this
sense, a README.md is basically a template that is interpreted by FUSE under a deductive engine to recover what is currently known and "should be there". I think it would be an interesting experiment; a Fable might want to discuss the idea with another philosopher Fable, should the idea seem fruitful. As for the maintainer, it's one of those ideas it just wants to record so it's not lost to oblivion.

witnesses.note: Open. Row 689: parked idea, natural home named as the clingo/ASP engine; philosopher-Fable consult commissioned same day covers this.
Open. Row 690: parked idea, philosopher-Fable consult commissioned same day covers this too.

phil consult DELIVERED af463a8 (design/PHIL-DEFEASIBLE-DECISIONS-AND-HYDRATED-DOCS.md, 569 lines incl. web-verified prior-art section) — both ideas discussed by the philosopher-Fable consult the maintainer suggested; ideas explored in writing, neither built.
Open. Row 698: the phil-consult-recommended degenerate experiment toward the FUSE idea, before any FUSE build — not yet claimed or closed.
